terms.definition: A Python project structure (also written `src/` layout or source layout) where the importable package lives inside a top-level `src/` directory; the package is only importable after installation.

A Python project structure (also called root layout or in-tree layout) where the importable package sits directly at the repository root, making it accessible from `cwd` without installation. The contrast to `src/` layout.

An installation produced by `pip install -e .` (development install) that points to the source tree rather than copying files, so edits are visible immediately while imports still go through the installed metadata.
The build backend's process of finding what to include in the distribution (also called auto-discovery). With `src/`, you tell it `where = ['src']` (setuptools) or `packages = ['src/<pkg>']` (hatch).
An import that works only because `cwd` happens to be on `sys.path` (also called an implicit or cwd-based import). The common pitfall in flat layouts; impossible in `src/` layouts.
